claims.statement: The equilateral triangle billiard is captured as a controlled reference model for boundary-induced admissible modes, spectral arithmetic, trace evidence, and recurrence; this is a theory-bridge artifact, not a theorem promotion or physical equivalence claim about cognition or agents.
boundary: No claim that cognition is literally a quantum billiard.
No claim that agent systems are unitary quantum systems.
No claim that spectral boundary grammar proves P vs NP.
No claim that all governed systems have exact revivals.

gates.expected: The bridge note remains documentation-only and does not promote analogy into theorem.
Future review should verify citations, formulas, and the exact scope of the Doncheski-Robinett reference model before any higher evidence classification.

non_claims.statement: The spectral boundary grammar note does not assert physical equivalence between quantum billiards, cognition, proof search, or governed agent runtimes.
The note does not claim progress on P vs NP, NP versus coNP, or any formal complexity separation; it is limited to verification/generation boundary methodology.
